observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the information presented in the different financial statements and to make adjustments, when necessary, to reconcile the amounts and accounts presented in the financial statements, in conformance with Section 27 of the International Public-Sector Accounting Standards.

We recommended that the Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
Validate the identified reconciling items and prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to restore the cash in bank balance and recognize the corresponding liability accounts.
Ensure that all book reconciling items in the Bank Reconciliation Statements are adjusted at year-end.

We recommended that the Mayor instruct the Municipal Accounting Office to effect the adjustment of the accounts as per suggested adjusting entry, viz.
We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant make sure that the conditions of the Memorandum of Agreement between parties are considered before recording the transactions, taking into account the proper accounts to be used.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
Hasten the gathering of the necessary documents required in the derecognition of the properties already turned-over to and accepted by the Barangays or other entities and secure an authority from the Sangguniang Bayan by way of a resolution to authorize the Mayor to execute a Deed of Donation (DOD) or a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA);
Draw a Journal Entry Voucher derecognizing the assets from the books of the Municipality and furnish the respective beneficiaries a copy of the JEV and MOA/DOD to facilitate the recording in the latter’s respective books; and
Provide the Office of the Auditor copies of the JEV together with the supporting documents taking up the derecognition of the above-cited PPE.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to:
Implement control measures in the issuance of cash tickets as provided in Section 59 (C) of the Local Treasury Operations Manual to preclude loss or misuse of government funds.
Ensure that Reports of Collections and Deposits for cash tickets collections includes information as to the breakdown of collections, the date of collections and the number of cash tickets issued per day.
We also recommended that the Management shall adhere to the laws, rules and regulations as these were promulgated for uniform application and to safeguard government resources.

We recommended that the Management submit justification for the under withholding of the Creditable Withholding Tax on the allowances and/or honorarium paid to judges, election officers, fire marshal, etc.
We likewise recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Accounting Office to deduct the applicable withholding tax on allowances, honorarium, and similar payments subject to withholding tax as required under Section 2.57.2 of RR No. 2-98, as amended by RR No. 11-2018 and RR No. 14-2018.

We recommended that the Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to install measures that will facilitate the timely submission of the financial statements, Disbursement Vouchers, Official Receipts, Liquidation Reports, and other financial reports within the prescribed periods provided in Section 4.1 of COA Circular No. 2010-001 and Section 7.2.1 (a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006.

We recommended that the Mayor direct the Municipal Planning and Development Office (MPDO) together with the Municipal Engineering Office (MEO) and other concerned offices to address the root cause of the non-implementation of the programmed development projects and come up with a concrete plan of avoiding the same lapses. Copy of the plan is to be furnished to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Engineering Office to submit to the General Services Office the Project Completion Reports and the Certificate of Acceptance and Turn-over of the completed infrastructure projects still recorded under the CIP Accounts and for the latter to submit the same to the Municipal Accounting Office.

Further, upon receipt of the necessary supporting documents from the aforementioned office, the Municipal Accounting Office should prepare adjustments to close the Construction in Progress accounts pertaining to the completed infrastructure projects and record the appropriate Property, plant and equipment accounts and to recognize the depreciation expenses.

Finally, we recommended the maintenance of Subsidiary Ledger Cards and Subsidiary Ledger for each infrastructure project recorded under CIP Accounts using the forms provided under the National Government Accounting Systems Manual to ensure the proper control in recording of transactions.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the implementing office in the granting of loan programs to conduct a regular monitoring and analysis of receivable accounts to ensure collection when due and demandable.

Likewise, the Municipal Accounting Office to conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis and validation of the existence of the receivables as prescribed under Section 7.0 of COA Circular No. 2016-005, dated December 19, 2016.

Finally, we also recommended the Municipal Mayor to file request for authority to write-off the dormant Other Receivables account to  duly supported by the documents prescribed under Section 8.0 of the same circular.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accounting Office and the General Services Office to establish an effective or practical system that would ensure the expeditious and regular reconciliation of records of both offices pertaining to the City’s Property, Plant and Equipment.
We further recommended augmenting the needed personnel and equipment in the General Services Office and Accounting Office that would facilitate in the reconciliation and efficient maintenance of records of the PPE accounts.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to enjoin the Municipal Accounting Office (MACCO) to transfer immediately the unexpended 30% QRF and 70% LDRRM Fund – MOOE to the Trust Fund books for the year 2019 and do the same at year-end for the years thereafter in compliance with the accounting and reporting guidelines as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-02, dated September 12, 2012 and Section 21 of RA No. 10121.

We further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer’s Office issue an official receipt for the transfer of funds through check disbursement from General Funds to Trust Funds and vice versa to acknowledge receipt in accordance with Section 68 of PD 1445.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accounting Office to maintain a separate subsidiary ledger of Unspent LDRRM Fund for each year to be incorporated in the computerized accounting system with the continuance of the manual recording as an additional control.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to enjoin the MDRRM Office and the Council to prepare and approved the LDRRMFIP in time before the passing of the Annual Appropriation Ordinance.
We further recommended to direct the Municipal Budget Office to ensure that the LDRRM PPAs’ names/descriptions and/or location are duly specified and categorized into the four (4) thematic areas in the approved Annual/Supplemental Budget.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to enjoin the MDRRMO (1) to strictly adhere with prescribed format of LDRRMFIP as presented in Annex A of COA Circular No 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012; (2) to ensure inclusion of projects/programs/activities chargeable under the unexpended balance of MDRRM Fund; and, (3) to incorporate the same in the Municipality’s Annual Investment Plan.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (LDRRMO) to submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRM Fund as of December 31, 2019 and to submit the same on a monthly basis as prescribed under COA Circular 2012-002.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accounting Office, in coordination with the Municipal Engineering Office and other concerned departments, to determine the status of the implementation of the programs and projects pertaining to fund transfers with non-moving balances and to either return or reprogram the unutilized balance of completed programs and projects in accordance with the Memorandum of Agreement between the LGU and source agency.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Engineering Office and other Offices concerned to submit written explanation for the discrepancies in the amount of contract price stated in the executed contract, approved Variation Order No. 1, and in the Statement of Work Accomplishments for the period covered November 12, 2014 to January 20, 2018 and to submit documents to support their statement.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct MENR Office to ensure strict implementation of the mandatory segregation at source, the “No Segregation, No Collection” policy of the Municipality, and the imposition of fines and penalties to individuals or establishments in violation thereof as provided under the existing Municipal Ordinances.

Likewise, we recommended an evaluation of the cause for the delay in the collection of garbage and determine immediate course of action to ensure on-time collection of garbage at the designated collection point in accordance with the schedule set in the Municipal Ordinances.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to prioritize the establishment of the sanitary landfill so that existing controlled dumpsite can be closed and rehabilitated.
We further recommended that the existing controlled dumpsite comply with minimum considerations/requirements as provided by the existing laws, rules and regulations until the sanitary landfill is established.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the Project-in-Charge to ensure implementation and completion of the infrastructure project within the year of the receipt of funds from the National Government Agencies and within the fiscal year it was budgeted to avoid possible forfeiture of the funding from the National Government upon full implementation of the Annual Cash-Based Budgeting and to ensure the economy and efficiency of the Municipality in the delivery of basic services and facilities to its constituents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor directs the LDRRM Officer to evaluate whether cost estimates of the PPAs not yet implemented are sufficient based on the prevailing prices in the market and to submit the findings and recommendations to LDDRM Council for proper action.

Further, we recommended the LDDRM Council to monitor the implementation of the PPAs funded under LDRRMF and evaluate whether there is a need to amend/revise/update the LDRRM Plan as per LDRRMO’s recommendation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor directs the Municipal Engineering Office and other implementing offices of the PPAs with unspent allotments under the 20% Development Fund to submit written justification for the non-utilization of the budgeted funds.
We further recommended the evaluation of the cause of delay and/or non-implementation and the formulation of proper course of action for immediate implementation of the same.

b. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to identify the actual uncollected Real Property and Special Education Fund Taxes, excluding the penalties, to establish the balance of RPT/SET receivable as at yearend. Consequently, the Municipal Accountant make necessary adjustments to present fairly in the financial statements the balance of the said receivable accounts. 

c. Compel the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile regularly their records so that any discrepancies noted could be immediately adjusted.

We recommended the management to:

a. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjustments on the omissions/errors to correct the reported balances of the affected accounts in the Financial Statements; and

b. Observe proper recording of account for the reliability of Financial Statement balances.

We recommended the management to:
 
 a. Observe and immediately consider the lacking minimum standards on disaster preparedness and mitigation to fully comply with the minimum standard requirements.

 b. Ensure to secure from harmful insects the DRRM storage area to protect the food and non-food supplies from damage that could ultimately result in loss of government funds and properties.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the management to:

a. Require the Municipal Engineer to submit a written explanation of why the municipality had rented construction equipment from private firm instead of from government agencies, as required in the above-stated provisions.

b. Adhere strictly to the conditions and requirements stipulated in the above-cited provisions before deciding on what mode of project implementation to be made.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 | The Municipal Engineer explained that they are unaware of the above regulation that was why they resorted to renting equipment from a private firm. She further explained that they contacted the Provincial Engineering Office and the Tagum City government to obtain access to their equipment but were unable to have it. However, the local chief executive had committed that in 2019, all of their projects will be implemented “by contract” to avoid the same mistakes.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to submit a written explanation to justify why they had awarded the procurement of labor to a pakyaw group who are directly connected to a regular licensed contractor, contrary to the above-mentioned regulation.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 | The Municipal Engineer explained that most of the team leaders are known foreman of the private contractor. However, they hired this pakyaw labor team leader since they have no on-going contracts with the private contractor at the moment. The management has stopped the said practice.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the management to ensure that Barangay leaders and the community where the projects are to be implemented are involved in the formation of pakyaw groups since they are more knowledgeable as to the needs and state of living of their constituents.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 | The Municipal Mayor had explained that she always encourages pakyaw team leaders from barangay people, to actively participate in any “by administration” projects, but sometimes they refuse to do so due to no available funds to pay for their laborers. Likewise, the Municipal Engineer commented that there Punong Barangays who refuse to help the LGU in finding people who will work for the project and most of the time would advise the engineer in charge to find their own laborers.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:

a. Require the Human Resource Development Officer (HRMO) to ensure that the provision prescribed for a minimum monthly net take pay of P5,000.00 under the GAA FY 2018 and onwards, be adhered to.

c. Advise the concerned employees to pay and/or transact directly with the private lending institutions, if they opted to avail private loans, so that their net take-home pay will comply with the provision of the GAA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the management to require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to strictly follow the procurement law (RA 9184) in order to avoid the legal sanctions for violating the law.
observations.managementAction: The BAC Secretariat had submitted the LGUs PMR in 2017.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the OIC-Municipal Accountant the following:

1.	To cause the analysis on the entries made relative to the recording of fund transfers to other funds, by the Bookkeepers of the three funds, in order to present the accurate balance of the two Intra-Agency Receivables and Payables accounts in the financial statements.

2.	To make sure to prepare the necessary adjustments for the affected accounts in the General Fund, SEF and TF Books and vice-versa so that these will reconcile with each other.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant had made analysis and reconciliation between the 2 reciprocal accounts.
observations.reason: There is still discrepancies between the 2 accounts per FS verification made.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management the following:

1.	To require the concerned officials liable and responsible for the above listed Notice of Disallowances (NDs) and Notice of Suspension (NS) to settle immediately the remaining disallowed amount of P323,750.00 and the suspended amount of P27,500.00, in order for the COA Central Office to cause the issuance of the Credit Notices for the aforementioned cash advances granted in CY 2011 up to 2014.

2.	Follow-up with the COA Intelligence/Confidential Fund Audit Unit the issuance of credit notices for the remaining balances of the transactions which were either disallowed or suspended in the audit.

3.	To strictly enforce the liquidation of cash advances within the prescribed period as provided in COA Circular No. 97-002.

4.	To withhold the salaries of the Accountable Officers still employed in the municipality who failed to liquidate their outstanding cash advances until the full amount is settled/liquidated.
observations.managementAction: The management had follow-up the status of their Confidential Fund (CF) with the COA CO. That there was partial refund made amounting to P120,000.00 for the ND issued for CF.  Implemented deduction from payroll of concerned personnel their unliquidated cash advances.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management and to the MSWM Focal person the following:

1.	To coordinate with the barangay government for the designation of enforcers that shall apprehend any persons who will violate the specific provision of the municipal ordinance.

2.	To make sure to assign collectors to collect the monthly dues of household as well as to collect from erring persons the fines and penalties due them based on the citation tickets issued by the enforcers, as these collections could help fund the various programs included in the Solid Waste Management Plan in order to achieved the objective of RA 9003.
observations.managementAction: The management had coordinated with the barangay government relative to collection on violation of RA 9003. The barangays are collecting fees and penalties in accordance with the tax ordinance made by them.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management the following:

1.	To prepare an accurate Annual Investment Plan that includes all the projects that are to be funded under the 20% Development Fund.

2.	To strictly implement projects on the year it was appropriated and ensure that utilization is in accordance with DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.
observations.managementAction: The management had implemented as recommended. Made its justification on the non-implementation of DF in 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to prepare and submit to the GPPB the duly approved Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR) within 14 calendar days after end of the semester, as prescribed in the IRR of RA 9184 in order for them to determine if procurement process had been done effectively.
observations.managementAction: The BAC had already complied with the recommendation.
The OIC-Municipal Accountant had demanded the personnel with unliquidated Cash Advances and had stopped granting CAs for those with unliquidated ones.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:

a) to direct the LSB to allocate budget to programs, projects/activities which are enumerated in Sections 100 and 272 of RA 7160 and DECs-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 01 s. 1998. 

b) Ensure that disbursements charged against the Special Education Fund are in accordance with the regulations on the utilization of SEF to avoid suspensions and/or disallowances in audit.
observations.managementAction: The LCE has to see to it that disbursements under the SEF be in accordance with the regulations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to prepare demand letters to the concerned Government Organizations (Liga ng mga Barangay and Vice-Mayor’s League) and to the two (2) Peoples Organizations (POs) (Philippine League of Secretaries and Association of Barangay Secretaries) for the immediate settlement/liquidations of the funds transferred to them.
observations.managementAction: The management had demanded the settlement/liquidation and these were already settled.
The projects appropriated under the 20% DF were all in accordance with the regulations of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular.
Informed that the PCL had requested to fully utilized all their printed receipts before issuing the required AF 51 and that it is beyond their control the issuance of the receipt.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to:

a) require the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to adopt a specific revenue-generating measures to promote and finance the sustainability/viability of the Solid Waste Management Plan as mandated in Section 12 (4) of RA 9003, by exerting an effort through massive collection of garbage fees, sale of baled cellophanes, vermicast and other by-products of waste materials.

b. Instruct the Environmental Resources Section to closely monitor the compliance on the various ordinances issued by Sangguniang Bayan relative to the implementation of the Solid Waste Management Plan as mandated in RA No. 9003 and to submit a report thereon showing the details on the no. of constituents/residents/business establishments issued with citation tickets in order to validate said compliance.
observations.managementAction: The management had implemented collection per their municipal ordinance on SWM.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended to require the Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the preparation and submission of the report on the sources and utilization of the LDRRMF to COA every end of each month pursuant to COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, for verification/audit purposes.
observations.managementAction: No appropriate action was made.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant still did not submit the Report on Sources & Utilization of LDRRMF to the Audit Team in 2016.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended to ensure that projects to be charged to the 20% Development Fund should have the nature of investment or capital expenditures such as concreting of roads etc. that would improve the socio-economic condition and environmental management in the delivery of services for the betterment of the constituents; Properly appropriate in the General Fund Proper expenses for the maintenance of streetlights, pesticides and seedlings and exclude the above program’s expenses in the 20% Development Fund; and to exercise prudence in the utilization of the 20% Development Fund as provided by the Local Government Code and Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-01.
observations.managementAction: The management had no action taken.
observations.reason: There are projects that should not be charged to 20% Development Fund.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:

      We  recommend  to  require  the  Municipal  Accounting  Office  and/or
personnel concerned to submit the monthly reports  within  the  reglementary
period of submission as prescribed under COA Circular No. 95-006  dated  May
18, 1995, COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 and Section  122  of
P.D. 1445 in order  for  us  to  have  a  timely  review  of  the  financial
transactions of your LGU.

Recommendations:


      We recommend to the management the following:


     1. To require the  OIC-Municipal  Accountant  to  ensure  that  demand
        letters are served  for the immediate settlement/liquidation of the
        financial/cash assistance granted to the  aforementioned  recipient
        barangays and make an effort to determine the  names  of  barangays
        that composed the lump amount of  P18,493.36.

     2. To make follow-up confirmation letter to the Provincial  Government
        of Davao del Norte/Tagum to determine if the balances reflected  in
        the  books  amounting  to  P79,143.62  of    LGU   Nabunturan   has
        corresponding contra account in the former’s books of accounts.
     3. To stop granting financial assistance to barangays which still have
        unliquidated balances.

Recommendations:


      We recommend to the management the following:


     1. To instruct the MAGRO and MCDC to revisit the provisions of the MOA
        in granting soft loans  and  make  sure  to  include  the  mode  of
        payments and the sanction/penalty to be imposed to the cooperatives
        Chairperson/representative who signed the MOA in  case  they  could
        not pay their obligations as scheduled and to send  demand  letters
        in order to recover the funds released for soft loan  from 2002  up
        to present.

     2. Direct the MAGRO to initiate the preparation of  Order  of  Payment
        for the refund of P20,000.00 from NAFGROMPCO in  which  they  found
        out that it was still intact and in dormant status.

     3. Instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to ensure that liquidation of
        financial assistance be attached  with  complete  documentation  as
        itemized above  and  to  stop  granting  additional  aid/assistance
        whenever the previous ones are not yet liquidated.

     4. Be prudent in the disbursement of government funds by ensuring that
        the borrower cooperatives are bona fide NGOs/POs according  to  the
        qualification provided in COA Circular No. 2007-001  dated  October
        25, 2016 in order to protect the resources of the government.

Recommendations:

      We recommend to direct the Local School Board (LSB) to strictly adhere
to the limitations set forth on the utilization of  Special  Education  Fund
(SEF) and to refrain from charging expenditures to  SEF  for  expenses  that
are  not  related  to  the  operation  and  maintenance  of  elementary  and
secondary public schools in order to avoid disallowance in audit.

future farmer transferees of these animals.

     2. To make sure to prepare the necessary adjustments for the affected

We recommend to expedite the reconciliation and submission of the reported PPEs to enable us to validate the existence, condition and propriety of the PPE accounts totaling P208,043,430.82 as of year-end and ensure to comply with the reglementary date of submission of RPCPPE that is not later than January 31, of every year.
observations.managementAction: The management has already submitted RPCPPE.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended to require the GAD Focal Person to submit the CY 2015 GAD Annual Accomplishment Report to the COA Audit Team as basis in the validation and evaluation of the implemented programs, projects and activities of the Local Government Unit.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Focal Person had already submitted GAD Annual Accomplishment Report.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended to direct the GSO to submit the perfected contracts/purchase orders together with all the supporting documents within 5 working days from its issuance as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
observations.managementAction: The management had already submitted the perfected contracts/purchase orders within the reglementary period of 5 working days.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended to prepare and submit to the GPPB the duly approved Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR) within 14 calendar days after end of the semester, as prescribed in the IRR of RA 9184 in order for them to determine if procurement process had been done effectively.
observations.managementAction: The LGU had prepared and submitted to the GPPB the PMR.
observations.reason: Validation of the PMR disclosed that the reported data were incomplete since not all of the procurement made/conducted were presented thereto and also it was submitted late.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended to require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Chairman to strictly observe and follow all the provisions of the procurement law in order to avoid the sanction specified in Section 70 of RA No. 9184 that could be imposed on the erring BAC personnel.
observations.managementAction: The BAC has carefully observed and adhere to the provisions of the procurement law.

observations.recommendation: 1. It is recommended to require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that complete documentation be attached to all funds transferred to barangays and to adhere strictly to the documentary requirements set forth under Item 3.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012; 2. Also, to direct the Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to effect the necessary adjustment on the misclassified account Due from LGUs amounting to P3,000.00 for the fund transferred to NASBATRICO, and to instruct the Barangay Bookkeepers to draw Journal Entry Voucher(JEV) to adjust the misclassified account totaling P233,949.75 of the concerned barangays from Due to Other Funds to Due to LGUs (418).
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office already adjusted the misclassified accounts and had observed the attachment of documentary requirements for fund transfers.

observations.recommendation: We recommend to require the Municipal Accountant for an issuance of an Order of payment to AOs for any outstanding cash advance a day after its liquidation has been due and enforce full refund of any unexpended cash advance. Stop granting additional cash advances to those officials or employees with un-liquidated cash advances; Strictly enforce the liquidation of cash advances within the prescribed period as provided in COA Circular No. 97-002; Withhold the salary of the Accountable Officers who are still employed in the municipality who failed to liquidate their outstanding cash advances until the full amount is settled/liquidated.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office already sent demand letters to the concerned AOs. Some AOs already refunded and others were deducted from their salaries. They also stopped granting of additional cash advances to those officials & employees with unliquidated cash advances.
observations.reason: Some of the concerned AOs have liquidated their cash advances.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended to follow the Minimum Requirements for the Operation of a Controlled Dumpsite in order to ensure protection of public health and environment; and to allocate funds for the construction and/or installation of fence around the vicinity of the sanitary landfill area.
observations.managementAction: The Environment and Management Bureau (EMB) had monitored the operation of their dumpsite and they already allocated funds for construction of fence.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended to direct the LSB to allocate budget to programs, projects/activities which are enumerated in Sections 100 and 272 of RA 7160 and DECs-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 01 s. 1998, Ensure that disbursements charged against the Special Education Fund are in accordance with the regulations on the utilization of SEF to avoid suspensions and/or disallowances in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Local School Board has no appropriate action.
observations.reason: The newly elected Local Chief Executive has committed to ensure that disbursement to be charged to SEF are those allowed per regulations. Also committed to restore the amount disbursed for expenses not allowed for SEF in CY 2015.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended to prepare demand letters to the concerned Government Organizations (Liga ng mga Barangay and Vice-Mayor’s League) and to the two (2) Peoples Organizations (Philippine League of Secretaries and Association of Barangay Secretaries) for the immediate settlement/liquidations of the funds transferred to them.
observations.managementAction: The management had demanded the settlement/liquidation.
observations.reason: The Liga ng mga Barangay and 2 POs have not yet liquidated their accounts.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended to require the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledgers for each program/projects funded from the 20% development fund to comply with Section 107 of the Manual on the NGAS.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office already maintained Subsidiary Ledgers for each program/projects funded from the 20% development fund.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended to require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that in the disbursement of funds for supplementary feeding, the actual no. of school days shall be considered in the payment, to avoid suspension and/or disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: The MSWDO had justified on the feeding even on weekends and was validated with the DSWD Regional Office XI in which we considered it as valid.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended to require the Philippine Councilor Leagues (PCL) and all other leagues organized by elective officials, to issue a Government Official Receipts (OR)/Accountable Form No. 51 (AF 51), for all collections made so that proper financial accountability could be established; Ensure that all future registration fee payments for PCL conducted trainings/seminars/activities be receipted with a Government Official Receipt which is AF No. 51, to avoid audit suspension and/or disallowance.
observations.managementAction: The management had justified that it is beyond their control the issuance of AF 51 by the PCL, however they informed the PCL ComVal Chapter about this matter.
observations.reason: Awaiting for the decision of the PCL Manila if the recommendation of COA has been acted.

observations.recommendation: 1. Create an Inventory Committee who shall be responsible for the conduct of annual physical count of the Agency's PPE and to submit immediately to COA Audit Team the required Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) duly reconciled with the Accounting records, to establish the validity of the reported balances. 2. Comply with the reglementary date of submission of the report which is not later than January 31, of every year as prescribed by the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) Manual for Local Government Units.
observations.managementAction: The management had conducted physical count of its PPE and had already submitted to the Audit Team the RPCPPE.

observations.recommendation: a. Require the Motor pool in-charge to submit to COA the Monthly Report on Utilization of Heavy Equipment together with the duly accomplished Driver’s Trip Tickets to determine if the heavy equipment were utilized efficiently. b. Strictly follow the equipment rental rates as stated in the Revenue Code in billing the private users to avoid issuance of notice of charge in the future, if found to be under billed.
observations.managementAction: The Motorpool unit had complied the submission of utilization report and driver's trip tickets.
